title: Give every form control a programmatic label
description: Associate each input with a real label element or aria-labelledby so the control's purpose is announced, never relying on placeholder text alone.
body: Use when building or changing any form field, checkbox, radio, or select. Do not use placeholder text as the only label; it disappears on input and many screen readers skip it. Actions: connect a visible <label for=id> or wrap the control, add aria-describedby for hints and error text, and verify the accessible name in the accessibility tree. Validation signals include an axe/screen-reader check showing every control has a non-empty name and errors are announced. Risk boundary: an unlabeled control blocks task completion for assistive-tech users; treat a missing programmatic label as a blocking defect, not cosmetic.
